summary:
The ε-phase in high-copper amalgams primarily consists of Cu6Sn5 due to increased copper concentration enhancing phase stability and improving material strength; hence option A should be selected based on these critical factors influencing dentistry practice today.
``` ```plaintext
The ε-phase in high-copper amalgams mainly comprises Cu6Sn5 because elevated copper levels promote the formation of this stable phase which enhances the material’s durability; therefore, selecting Option A is supported by current scientific understanding.
```
